message:
Add cfg(target_os = "macos") guards and rust-toolchain.toml

The debug_events and event_tap binaries use macOS-only APIs
(CGEventTap from core_graphics, CFRunLoop from core_foundation,
osascript for volume control). Previously these would fail to compile
on non-macOS platforms with cryptic linker errors.

Changes to src/bin/debug_events.rs and src/bin/event_tap.rs:
- Gate all macOS-specific imports, constants, statics, and functions
  behind #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
- Add a #[cfg(not(target_os = "macos"))] main() stub that prints a
  clear error message and exits with code 1
- This allows the crate to compile on Linux/Windows (e.g. for CI)
  while making it obvious these binaries are macOS-only at runtime

New file rust-toolchain.toml:
- Pin the project to nightly Rust channel
- Include rustfmt and clippy components

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
